verses.text: The king rejoices in the LORD's strength, for God has granted his heart's desire and set a crown of gold on his head. Victory is owned as God's gift. The blessings we win in battle or in life come down from the hand that crowns.

The king rejoices in the LORD's strength, for God has granted his heart's desire and set a crown of gold on his head. Victory is owned as God's gift. The blessings we win in battle or in life come down from the hand that crowns.

The king rejoices in the LORD's strength, for God has granted his heart's desire and set a crown of gold on his head. Victory is owned as God's gift. The blessings we win in battle or in life come down from the hand that crowns.

He asked for life, and God gave length of days, glory, and lasting blessedness in His presence. God answers beyond the request. He often gives more than we ask, making His servant glad with the joy of His countenance.

He asked for life, and God gave length of days, glory, and lasting blessedness in His presence. God answers beyond the request. He often gives more than we ask, making His servant glad with the joy of His countenance.

He asked for life, and God gave length of days, glory, and lasting blessedness in His presence. God answers beyond the request. He often gives more than we ask, making His servant glad with the joy of His countenance.

Because the king trusts in the LORD, he shall not be moved, while God's hand finds out all His enemies. Trust is the secret of stability. The same God who upholds the faithful will surely overcome their foes.

Because the king trusts in the LORD, he shall not be moved, while God's hand finds out all His enemies. Trust is the secret of stability. The same God who upholds the faithful will surely overcome their foes.

Because the king trusts in the LORD, he shall not be moved, while God's hand finds out all His enemies. Trust is the secret of stability. The same God who upholds the faithful will surely overcome their foes.

Those who plotted evil against the king are undone, for they could not prevail; so the people sing, 'Be thou exalted, LORD.' Failed malice ends in God's praise. When the enemies of His cause are scattered, His people exalt His power.
